summary: McKenzie congratulates E-junkie, a payment processing service for small software vendors, on reaching one million dollars in monthly transaction volume. He notes his own modest contribution to that figure and frames it with self-deprecating humor about his small share of their business.
key_points: E-junkie crossed $1 million/month in transaction processing, a milestone for a small vendor-focused service
McKenzie positions himself as both a satisfied customer and a contributor to their growth
His own sales (~$425 in 15 days) represent roughly 0.1% of E-junkie's volume, highlighting the long tail of small software businesses
money_quotes: Now I just need to get back to the business of getting them closer to two million a month.
At $425 in 15 days, I'm almost a whole tenth of a percent of their business. Woohoo!
key_insight: Even tiny software businesses collectively add up to meaningful transaction volume for the platforms that serve them.
